title: Latency, Throughput, Memory, Caching, Load, and Cost · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent team receives a different corpus, query mix, relevance policy, language set, access model, latency budget, harm profile, and evidence duty and transfers latency, throughput, memory, caching, load, and cost into a new RAG capacity and cost envelope. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of RAG capacity and cost envelope with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, an…
objectives: Allocate deadlines across admission, query processing, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citation, streaming, and cleanup with p50, p95, and p99 evidence.
Bound concurrent queries, embedding batches, index searches, reranks, model calls, queues, tenants, retries, and overload shedding.
Measure embedding, retrieval, reranking, context, prefix, and answer cache hit rate, saved work, staleness, authorization, invalidation, and quality.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Rag Stage Latency Budget
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab RAG engineering team handles case 7264 during a public-benefits policy change. Build deterministic Python evidence to budget stage latency, throughput, memory, provider work, cache validity, and cost without hiding quality loss; retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity; then replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Allocate deadlines across admission, query processing, retrieval, reranking, context, generation, citation, streaming, and cleanup with p50, p95, and p99 evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Load Fault Capacity Test
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-load-fault-capacity-test-1bilyh" that uses case 8646 to budget stage latency, throughput, memory, provider work, cache validity, and cost without hiding quality loss. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Load Fault Capacity Test.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rag Load Fault Capacity Test into observable performance evidence. For RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Load Fault Capacity Test, keep Rag Load Fault Capacity Test connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-load-fault-capacity-test-1bilyh
# Competency: rag-load-fault-capacity-test.
# Case 8646: a multilingual support escalation.
# Operating constraint: preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation.
# Changed case: replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall.
def evidence_rag_load_fault_capacity_test_1bilyh(queries_per_second: int = 24, retrieval_ms: int = 45, rerank_ms: int = 38, generation_ms: int = 420, deadline_ms: int = 650, cost_microunits: int = 950, cache_hit: bool = False) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1bilyh = "1bilyh-hylib1-8646"
    assert evidence_variant_1bilyh
    evidence_axes_1bilyh = (
        "corpus1bilyh",
        "queryhylib1",
        "tenant8646",
        "index1bilyh8646",
        "prompthylib18646",
        "evaluation86461bilyh",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1bilyh)) == 6
    if queries_per_second <= 0 or min(retrieval_ms, rerank_ms, generation_ms) < 0:
        return False, 0, "capacity-input"
    total_ms = retrieval_ms + rerank_ms + generation_ms
    if total_ms > deadline_ms:
        return False, total_ms, "latency-budget"
    if cost_microunits < 0:
        return False, cost_microunits, "cost-accounting"
    adjusted_cost = cost_microunits // 2 if cache_hit else cost_microunits
    assert adjusted_cost <= cost_microunits
    return True, adjusted_cost, "capacity-envelope"
